meta.name: CFS Chalder Fatigue Scale
meta.description: The Chalder Fatigue Scale is an 11-item questionnaire measuring the severity of physical and mental fatigue. It is widely used in clinical and research settings to assess fatigue in various populations.
meta.author: Chalder, Berelowitz, Pawlikowska, Watts, Wessely, Wright & Wallace (1993)
meta.reference: Chalder T, Berelowitz G, Pawlikowska T, Watts L, Wessely S, Wright D, Wallace EP. Development of a fatigue scale. J Psychosom Res. 1993;37(2):147-153.
meta.instruction: We would like to know more about any problems you have had with feeling tired, weak, or lacking in energy in the last month. Please answer ALL the questions by selecting the answer which applies to you most closely.

questions.text: Do you have problems with tiredness?
Do you need to rest more?
Do you feel sleepy or drowsy?
Do you have problems starting things?
Do you have less strength in your muscles?
Do you have problems thinking clearly?
Do you make slips of the tongue when speaking?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your fatigue levels are within the normal range. No significant fatigue concerns detected. This indicates that you are currently experiencing minimal physical discomfort, with somatic symptoms within the normal range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good sleep hygiene and regular physical activity
Keep a balanced diet and stay hydrated
Monitor your energy levels if you notice changes
Continue maintaining healthy lifestyle habits
Get regular check-ups to maintain physical health

ranges.label: Mild Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing mild fatigue that may slightly impact your daily functioning. Mild physical discomfort is common. Rest and healthy lifestyle habits can often help alleviate these symptoms.
suggestions: Ensure you are getting adequate sleep (7-9 hours per night)
Review your daily schedule for opportunities to rest
Consider light exercise to boost energy levels
If fatigue persists, consult a healthcare provider
Pay attention to your body signals and rest when needed

ranges.label: Moderate Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing moderate fatigue that may be affecting your work, social activities, and overall quality of life. At this level, physical symptoms may cause discomfort and affect your daily activities and emotional state.
suggestions: Consult a healthcare provider to rule out medical causes
Prioritize rest and energy management throughout the day
Consider pacing strategies to manage your activities
Review medications and lifestyle factors that may contribute to fatigue
Consider consulting a doctor for a physical examination

ranges.label: Severe Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing severe fatigue that likely significantly impacts your daily functioning and quality of life. Your somatic symptom scores are elevated, and these discomforts may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Both medical and psychological evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Seek medical evaluation promptly to identify underlying causes
Discuss treatment options with your healthcare provider
Practice strict energy conservation and pacing
Consider psychological support if fatigue is linked to mood or stress
Consult a doctor for a comprehensive physical examination as soon as possible

physical.label: Normal Physical Fatigue
physical.description: Your physical fatigue is within the normal range. This indicates that you are currently experiencing minimal physical discomfort, with somatic symptoms within the normal range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy lifestyle habits
Get regular check-ups to maintain physical health
Keep up moderate exercise and a balanced diet

physical.label: Mild Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing mild physical fatigue. Ensure adequate rest and physical activity. Mild physical discomfort is common. Rest and healthy lifestyle habits can often help alleviate these symptoms.
suggestions: Pay attention to your body signals and rest when needed
Maintain regular exercise and healthy eating habits
Learn relaxation techniques to relieve physical tension
Ensure adequate sleep
If symptoms persist, consult a doctor to rule out physical conditions

physical.label: Moderate Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing notable physical fatigue. Consider consulting a healthcare provider. At this level, physical symptoms may cause discomfort and affect your daily activities and emotional state.
suggestions: Consider consulting a doctor for a physical examination
Understand the relationship between mind and body
Learn stress management and relaxation techniques
Maintain moderate exercise and social activities
Keep a symptom diary to help track changes

physical.label: Severe Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing severe physical fatigue. Medical evaluation is recommended. Your somatic symptom scores are elevated, and these discomforts may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Both medical and psychological evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consult a doctor for a comprehensive physical examination as soon as possible
Consider how physical and mental health may be interconnected
Develop a comprehensive management plan under professional guidance
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
Ensure adequate social support

mental.label: Normal Mental Fatigue
mental.description: Your mental fatigue is within the normal range. This indicates that you are currently experiencing minimal physical discomfort, with somatic symptoms within the normal range.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy lifestyle habits
Get regular check-ups to maintain physical health
Keep up moderate exercise and a balanced diet

mental.label: Mild Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing mild mental fatigue. Ensure adequate rest and cognitive breaks. Mild physical discomfort is common. Rest and healthy lifestyle habits can often help alleviate these symptoms.
suggestions: Pay attention to your body signals and rest when needed
Maintain regular exercise and healthy eating habits
Learn relaxation techniques to relieve physical tension
Ensure adequate sleep
If symptoms persist, consult a doctor to rule out physical conditions

mental.label: Moderate Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing notable mental fatigue that may affect concentration and memory. At this level, physical symptoms may cause discomfort and affect your daily activities and emotional state.
suggestions: Consider consulting a doctor for a physical examination
Understand the relationship between mind and body
Learn stress management and relaxation techniques
Maintain moderate exercise and social activities
Keep a symptom diary to help track changes

mental.label: Severe Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing severe mental fatigue. Consider seeking professional evaluation. Your somatic symptom scores are elevated, and these discomforts may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Both medical and psychological evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consult a doctor for a comprehensive physical examination as soon as possible
Consider how physical and mental health may be interconnected
Develop a comprehensive management plan under professional guidance
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
Ensure adequate social support
